items.title: Texas attorney general’s office under scrutiny for letting donors use hotel room bookings
items.desc: The attorney general’s office reallocated taxpayer-funded rooms to donors and other private citizens, some of whom initially failed to cover the cost of the stay.

items.title: At least 175 measles cases reported in Texas this year, most of them in West Texas Detention Facility
items.desc: Texas has added 28 more measles cases in the last two weeks. Most infections so far this year have occurred inside West Texas Detention Facility in Hudspeth County.

items.title: Tyler embraces a families-first approach as it grows faster than any other East Texas city
items.desc: Tyler has spent the last decade remaking its parks and sidewalks to allure families. At the same time, a constellation of groups are helping parents take care of their families.

items.title: Judge will allow Attorney General Ken Paxton to withdraw from representing comptroller’s office in voucher case
items.desc: The Houston federal judge said Paxton’s office can pull out of the case as soon as acting Comptroller Kelly Hancock finds new lawyers. The unusual request comes amid a bubbling feud between the two Republicans.

items.title: Hands-on telehealth helps reach rural Texas communities
items.desc: A new approach aims to tailor telehealth to the realities of an older, rural population by offering a physical place with reliable connectivity and an on-site nurse.

items.title: Oil Surges on Fears of Prolonged War
items.desc: U.S. President Donald Trump is coming under increasing international pressure after he pledged in a primetime speech to continue the war on Iran, sparking further turmoil in energy markets.

items.title: Baker Hughes, Naftogaz to Explore Energy Cooperation
items.desc: 'The memorandum facilitates joint work on the assessment and preparation of potential projects in exploration and production, oil and gas transportation and storage, refining, petrochemicals and power generation'.

items.title: StanChart Warns OPEC+ Could Abandon Cuts at Next Meeting
items.desc: Standard Chartered Bank Energy Research Head Emily Ashford warned that the next OPEC+ meeting on April 5 'could result in abandonment of voluntary output cuts and compensation cuts'.

items.desc: ‘DAN DA DAN,’ ‘Gachiakuta,’ ‘My Hero Academia,’ ‘Takopi's Original Sin,’ ‘The Apothecary Diaries,’ and ‘The Summer Hikaru Died’ will vie for Anime of the Year; live Tokyo ceremony set for May 23.

items.desc: The Oscar-winning filmmaker unveils his first venture into animation, set for a 2027 theatrical release, backed by CJ ENM, Penture Invest, and Pathé, with DNEG involved in the 3DCG work.
